primary_accounting_issue: Which IFRS hedge accounting models are relevant in consolidated financial statements for foreign exchange risk on intragroup dividend-related positions, given the consolidation elimination rules and the IFRS 9 restrictions on intragroup hedged items.

primary_authority.reason: These paragraphs directly govern which items may be designated as hedged items in consolidated financial statements, including the limited intragroup foreign currency exceptions.

supporting_authority.reason: This paragraph establishes that intragroup balances, income, expenses and cash flows are eliminated in full on consolidation, which constrains hedge designation analysis for intragroup dividends and receivables.

This paragraph confirms that dividends are recognised in separate financial statements when the right to receive them is established, helping distinguish separate-financial-statement accounting from consolidation analysis.

This paragraph reinforces that intragroup transactions and balances are eliminated in consolidated financial statements, except for the investment entity exception, supporting the consolidation boundary.

These paragraphs provide the distinct IFRS hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk on net investments in foreign operations, which is a separate top-level hedge model relevant to the FX-risk landscape.

peripheral_authority.reason: These provisions address consolidation scope, timing and attribution matters, not the identification of hedge accounting models for intragroup FX risk.
These are disclosure requirements and do not determine the top-level hedge accounting approaches.
These provisions relate to reduced disclosures and presentation alternatives, not the governing hedge accounting models for this issue.
These are related-party disclosure rules and do not establish the hedge accounting model.
These provisions address scope, derecognition and other hedge topics, but not the key consolidated-FX intragroup hedged-item distinction central to this issue.
These paragraphs concern cash flow statement classification and financing-liability reconciliations, not hedge model identification.
These paragraphs address non-cash distributions to owners, not hedge accounting for foreign currency risk in consolidation.
Apart from dividend recognition in separate financial statements, these paragraphs address separate-financial-statement preparation rather than hedge model identification in consolidation.
These provisions deal with financial instrument presentation, dividends and offsetting rather than hedge accounting model selection for intragroup FX exposures.
This Interpretation concerns tax-status changes and is unrelated to the hedge accounting issue.
This Interpretation concerns members' shares in co-operatives and is unrelated to the hedge accounting issue.

treatment_families.family: General hedge accounting models for foreign currency risk in consolidated financial statements
Net investment hedge accounting model for foreign currency exposures in a group
Consolidation elimination framework that constrains whether an intragroup item remains a qualifying hedged item in the consolidated financial statements

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: This is a top-level IFRS hedge accounting model potentially relevant when foreign exchange risk is considered on a recognised monetary position in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: This is a top-level IFRS hedge accounting model potentially relevant to foreign exchange risk on forecast or variability-based exposures, including the intragroup FX exceptions addressed in IFRS 9.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: This is a distinct top-level IFRS foreign currency hedge accounting model for group-level exposures and is separately articulated in IFRS guidance.
